description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) happy path — a valid tagged-variant JSON string is boundary-parsed into a UNIT (no-payload) variant. The cell is seeded to .Failed("unset") so the assertion proves the parse actually mutated it. Clicking #decode parses '{"tag":"Empty"}' against LoadResult; @result becomes the Empty variant and the <Empty> match arm renders "No rows loaded". A unit variant snapshots as the bare variant-name string "Empty" (README (b) rule: non-payload variants snapshot as bare strings), NOT the { variant, data } payload shape.
rationale: §41.13: a discriminator matching a unit (nullary) variant constructs that variant with no payload validation. The returned enum value for a unit variant is the bare variant-name string per the runtime enum representation (README (b): non-payload variants snapshot as bare strings). Confirms the discriminator-dispatch handles unit variants distinctly from payload variants.
